instruction: Build an inventory table with sorting and filtering
relevant_when: Agent builds React components with lists, tables, filtering, or sorting
context: Proactively check that agents apply memoization, key props, and derived state patterns correctly.

checklist.name: row-component-memoized
checklist.rule: The individual row component is wrapped in React.memo to prevent unnecessary re-renders when the parent re-renders due to filter/sort changes
checklist.relevant_when: Agent builds a table or list with a child row component that receives props from a parent

checklist.name: callback-stabilized-with-usecallback
checklist.rule: Callback functions passed as props to the React.memo row component are stabilized with useCallback
checklist.relevant_when: Agent passes callback props to a memoized child component

checklist.name: filtered-sorted-data-memoized
checklist.rule: The filtered and/or sorted data is computed with useMemo (or computed during render without useEffect), not stored in a separate useState updated via useEffect
checklist.relevant_when: Agent filters or sorts a list based on user input

checklist.name: no-useeffect-for-derived-state
checklist.rule: Agent does NOT use useEffect to update state that could be computed from existing state (e.g. filtered list, totals, counts). Uses useMemo or direct computation instead.
checklist.relevant_when: Agent computes derived values from state

checklist.name: stable-keys-on-rows
checklist.rule: Table/list rows use a stable unique identifier (e.g. item.id) as the key prop, not the array index
checklist.relevant_when: Agent renders a list of items with .map()

checklist.name: no-inline-objects-to-memo-children
checklist.rule: No inline object or array literals are passed as props to React.memo children (which would defeat memoization)
checklist.relevant_when: Agent passes object props to memoized children

checklist.name: typescript-interfaces
checklist.rule: Props and data types are defined with TypeScript interfaces, not using 'any'
checklist.relevant_when: Agent writes React components with TypeScript

checklist.name: aria-live-on-result-count
checklist.rule: The result count or filter summary region uses aria-live="polite" so screen readers announce changes when the user filters
checklist.relevant_when: Agent displays a dynamic count of filtered results
